children.text: the name, address, and registration number of each person who is registered under any provision of subtitle D (and, in the case of a registered terminal operator, the address of each terminal operated by such operator), and

children.label: B
children.text: the registration status of any person.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information in levies on Financial Management Service
children.label: A Disclosure of return information in levies on Financial Management Service
i
children.text: return information, including taxpayer identity information,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of any unpaid liability under this title (including penalties and interest), and

children.label: iii
children.text: the type of tax and tax period to which such unpaid liability relates.

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: B Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.text: Return information disclosed under subparagraph (A) may be used by officers and employees of the Financial Management Service only for the purpose of, and to the extent necessary in, transferring levied funds in satisfaction of the levy, maintaining appropriate agency records in regard to such levy or the release thereof, notifying the taxpayer and the agency certifying such payment that the levy has been honored, or in the defense of any litigation ensuing from the honor of such levy.

children.label: i
children.text: any Federal payment (other than a payment for which eligibility is based on the income or assets (or both) of a payee) certified to the Financial Management Service for disbursement, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any other payment which is certified to the Financial Management Service for disbursement and which the Secretary designates by published notice.

children.heading: Disclosure of information to administer section 6311
children.label: 9 Disclosure of information to administer section 6311
children.text: The Secretary may disclose returns or return information to financial institutions and others to the extent the Secretary deems necessary for the administration of section 6311. Disclosures of information for purposes other than to accept payments by checks or money orders shall be made only to the extent authorized by written procedures promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Disclosure of certain returns and return information to certain prison officials
children.label: 10 Disclosure of certain returns and return information to certain prison officials

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under such procedures as the Secretary may prescribe, the Secretary may disclose to officers and employees of the Federal Bureau of Prisons and of any State agency charged with the responsibility for administration of prisons any returns or return information with respect to individuals incarcerated in Federal or State prison systems whom the Secretary has determined may have filed or facilitated the filing of a false or fraudulent return to the extent that the Secretary determines that such disclosure is necessary to permit effective Federal tax administration.

children.heading: Disclosure to contractor-run prisons
children.label: B Disclosure to contractor-run prisons
children.text: Under such procedures as the Secretary may prescribe, the disclosures authorized by subparagraph (A) may be made to contractors responsible for the operation of a Federal or State prison on behalf of such Bureau or agency.

children.heading: Restrictions on use of disclosed information
children.label: C Restrictions on use of disclosed information
children.text: Any return or return information received under this paragraph shall be used only for the purposes of and to the extent necessary in taking administrative action to prevent the filing of false and fraudulent returns, including administrative actions to address possible violations of administrative rules and regulations of the prison facility and in administrative and judicial proceedings arising from such administrative actions.

children.heading: Restrictions on redisclosure and disclosure to legal representatives
children.label: D Restrictions on redisclosure and disclosure to legal representatives

children.heading: Restrictions on redisclosure
children.label: i Restrictions on redisclosure
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), any officer, employee, or contractor of the Federal Bureau of Prisons or of any State agency charged with the responsibility for administration of prisons shall not disclose any information obtained under this paragraph to any person other than an officer or employee or contractor of such Bureau or agency personally and directly engaged in the administration of prison facilities on behalf of such Bureau or agency.

children.heading: Disclosure to legal representatives
children.label: ii Disclosure to legal representatives
children.text: The returns and return information disclosed under this paragraph may be disclosed to the duly authorized legal representative of the Federal Bureau of Prisons, State agency, or contractor charged with the responsibility for administration of prisons, or of the incarcerated individual accused of filing the false or fraudulent return who is a party to an action or proceeding described in subparagraph (C), solely in preparation for, or for use in, such action or proceeding.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to Department of State for purposes of passport revocation under section 7345
children.label: 11 Disclosure of return information to Department of State for purposes of passport revocation under section 7345
i
children.text: the taxpayer identity information with respect to such taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such seriously delinquent tax debt.

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: Return information disclosed under subparagraph (A) may be used by officers and employees of the Department of State for the purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, carrying out the requirements of section 32101 of the FAST Act.

children.heading: Qualified tax collection contractors
children.label: 12 Qualified tax collection contractors
children.text: Persons providing services pursuant to a qualified tax collection contract under section 6306 may, if speaking to a person who has identified himself or herself as having the name of the taxpayer to which a tax receivable (within the meaning of such section) relates, identify themselves as contractors of the Internal Revenue Service and disclose the business name of the contractor, and the nature, subject, and reason for the contact. Disclosures under this paragraph shall be made only in such situations and under such conditions as have been approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may disclose, to any individual providing information relating to any purpose described in paragraph (1) or (2) of section 7623(a), return information related to the investigation of any taxpayer with respect to whom the individual has provided such information, but only to the extent that such disclosure is necessary in obtaining information, which is not otherwise reasonably available, with respect to the correct determination of tax liability for tax, or the amount to be collected with respect to the enforcement of any other provision of this title.

children.label: i
children.text: Not later than 60 days after a case for which the individual has provided information has been referred for an audit or examination, a notice with respect to such referral.

children.label: ii
children.text: Not later than 60 days after a taxpayer with respect to whom the individual has provided information has made a payment of tax with respect to tax liability to which such information relates, a notice with respect to such payment.

children.label: I
children.text: information on the status and stage of any investigation or action related to such information, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a determination of the amount of any award under section 7623(b), the reasons for such determination.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information for purposes of cybersecurity and the prevention of identity theft tax refund fraud
children.label: 14 Disclosure of return information for purposes of cybersecurity and the prevention of identity theft tax refund fraud

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under such procedures and subject to such conditions as the Secretary may prescribe, the Secretary may disclose specified return information to specified ISAC participants to the extent that the Secretary determines such disclosure is in furtherance of effective Federal tax administration relating to the detection or prevention of identity theft tax refund fraud, validation of taxpayer identity, authentication of taxpayer returns, or detection or prevention of cybersecurity threats.

children.label: I
children.text: any person designated by the Secretary as having primary responsibility for a function performed with respect to the information sharing and analysis center described in section 2003(a) of the Taxpayer First Act, and

children.label: II
children.text: any person subject to the requirements of section 7216 and which is a participant in such information sharing and analysis center.

children.heading: Information sharing agreement
children.label: ii Information sharing agreement
children.text: Such term shall not include any person unless such person has entered into a written agreement with the Secretary setting forth the terms and conditions for the disclosure of information to such person under this paragraph, including requirements regarding the protection and safeguarding of such information by such person.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of such return filed electronically, the internet protocol address, device identification, email domain name, speed of completion, method of authentication, refund method, and such other return information related to the electronic filing characteristics of such return as the Secretary may identify for purposes of this subclause, and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of such return prepared by a tax return preparer, identifying information with respect to such tax return preparer, including the preparer taxpayer identification number and electronic filer identification number of such preparer,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a return which is in connection with a case of a identity theft refund fraud which has been confirmed by the Secretary (pursuant to such procedures as the Secretary may provide), the information referred to in subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i), the name and taxpayer identification number of the taxpayer as it appears on the return, and any bank account and routing information provided for making a refund in connection with such return, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of any cybersecurity threat to the Internal Revenue Service, information similar to the information described in subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i) with respect to such threat.

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: D Restriction on use of disclosed information
I
children.text: performing the function such person is designated to perform under such subparagraph,

children.label: II
children.text: facilitating disclosures authorized under subparagraph (A) to persons described in subparagraph (B)(i)(II), and

children.label: III
children.text: facilitating disclosures authorized under subsection (d) to participants in such information sharing and analysis center.

children.heading: Return preparers
children.label: ii Return preparers
children.text: Any return information received by a person described in subparagraph (B)(i)(II) shall be treated for purposes of section 7216 as information furnished to such person for, or in connection with, the preparation of a return of the tax imposed under chapter 1.

children.heading: Data protection and safeguards
children.label: E Data protection and safeguards
children.text: Return information disclosed under this paragraph shall be subject to such protections and safeguards as the Secretary may require in regulations or other guidance or in the written agreement referred to in subparagraph (B)(ii). Such written agreement shall include a requirement that any unauthorized access to information disclosed under this paragraph, and any breach of any system in which such information is held, be reported to the Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration.

children.heading: Disclosures to Social Security Administration to identify tax receivables not eligible for collection pursuant to qualified tax collection contracts
children.label: 15 Disclosures to Social Security Administration to identify tax receivables not eligible for collection pursuant to qualified tax collection contracts
children.text: In the case of any individual involved with a tax receivable which the Secretary has identified for possible collection pursuant to a qualified tax collection contract (as defined in section 6306(b)), the Secretary may disclose the taxpayer identity and date of birth of such individual to officers, employees, and contractors of the Social Security Administration to determine if such tax receivable is not eligible for collection pursuant to such a qualified tax collection contract by reason of section 6306(d)(3)(E).

children.heading: Disclosure of returns and return information for purposes other than tax administration
children.label: l Disclosure of returns and return information for purposes other than tax administration

children.heading: Disclosure of certain returns and return information to Social Security Administration and Railroad Retirement Board
children.label: 1 Disclosure of certain returns and return information to Social Security Administration and Railroad Retirement Board
A
children.text: taxes imposed by chapters 2, 21, and 24, to the Social Security Administration for purposes of its administration of the Social Security Act;

children.label: B
children.text: a plan to which part I of subchapter D of chapter 1 applies, to the Social Security Administration for purposes of carrying out its responsibility under section 1131 of the Social Security Act, limited, however to return information described in section 6057(d); and

children.label: C
children.text: taxes imposed by chapter 22, to the Railroad Retirement Board for purposes of its administration of the Railroad Retirement Act.

children.heading: Disclosure of returns and return information to the Department of Labor and Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
children.label: 2 Disclosure of returns and return information to the Department of Labor and Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
children.text: The Secretary may, upon written request, furnish returns and return information to the proper officers and employees of the Department of Labor and the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation for purposes of, but only to the extent necessary in, the administration of titles I and IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.

children.heading: Disclosure that applicant for Federal loan has tax delinquent account
children.label: 3 Disclosure that applicant for Federal loan has tax delinquent account

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon written request, the Secretary may disclose to the head of the Federal agency administering any included Federal loan program whether or not an applicant for a loan under such program has a tax delinquent account.

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: Any disclosure under subparagraph (A) shall be made only for the purpose of, and to the extent necessary in, determining the creditworthiness of the applicant for the loan in question.

children.heading: Included Federal loan program defined
children.label: C Included Federal loan program defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “included Federal loan program” means any program under which the United States or a Federal agency makes, guarantees, or insures loans.

children.heading: Disclosure of returns and return information for use in personnel or claimant representative matters
children.label: 4 Disclosure of returns and return information for use in personnel or claimant representative matters
i
children.text: to an employee or former employee of the Department of the Treasury, or to the duly authorized legal representative of such employee or former employee, who is or may be a party to any administrative action or proceeding affecting the personnel rights of such employee or former employee; or

children.label: ii
children.text: to any person, or to the duly authorized legal representative of such person, whose rights are or may be affected by an administrative action or proceeding under , United States Code,section 330 of title 31

children.label: B
children.text: to officers and employees of the Department of the Treasury for use in any action or proceeding described in subparagraph (A), or in preparation for such action or proceeding, to the extent necessary to advance or protect the interests of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: carrying out, in accordance with an agreement entered into pursuant to section 232 of the Social Security Act, an effective return processing program; or

children.label: B
children.text: providing information regarding the mortality status of individuals for epidemiological and similar research in accordance with section 1106(d) of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to Federal, State, tribal, and local child support enforcement agencies
children.label: 6 Disclosure of return information to Federal, State, tribal, and local child support enforcement agencies

children.heading: Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.label: A Return information from Internal Revenue Service
i
children.text: available return information from the master files of the Internal Revenue Service relating to the social security account number (or numbers, if the individual involved has more than one such number), address, filing status, amounts and nature of income, and the number of dependents reported on any return filed by, or with respect to, any individual with respect to whom child support obligations are sought to be established or enforced pursuant to the provisions of part D of title IV of the Social Security Act and with respect to any individual to whom such support obligations are owing, and

children.label: ii
children.text: available return information reflected on any return filed by, or with respect to, any individual described in clause (i) relating to the amount of such individual’s gross income (as defined in section 61) or consisting of the names and addresses of payors of such income and the names of any dependents reported on such return, but only if such return information is not reasonably available from any other source.

children.heading: Disclosure to certain agents
children.label: B Disclosure to certain agents
children.text: The information disclosed to any child support enforcement agency under subparagraph (A) with respect to any individual with respect to whom child support obligations are sought to be established or enforced may be disclosed by such agency to any agent of such agency which is under contract with such agency to carry out the purposes described in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: C Restriction on disclosure
children.text: Information may be disclosed under this paragraph only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing and collecting child support obligations from, and locating, individuals owing such obligations.

children.heading: State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency
children.label: D State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency
i
children.text: Any agency of a State or political subdivision thereof operating pursuant to a plan described in section 454 of the Social Security Act which has been approved by the Secretary of Health and Human Services under part D of title IV of such Act.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any child support enforcement agency of an Indian tribe or tribal organization receiving a grant under section 455(f) of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to Federal, State, and local agencies administering certain programs under the Social Security Act, the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, or title 38, United States Code, or certain housing assistance programs
children.label: 7 Disclosure of return information to Federal, State, and local agencies administering certain programs under the Social Security Act, the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, or title 38, United States Code, or certain housing assistance programs

children.heading: Return information from Social Security Administration
children.label: A Return information from Social Security Administration
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall, upon written request, disclose return information from returns with respect to net earnings from self-employment (as defined in section 1402), wages (as defined in section 3121(a) or 3401(a)), and payments of retirement income, which have been disclosed to the Social Security Administration as provided by paragraph (1) or (5) of this subsection, to any Federal, State, or local agency administering a program listed in subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.label: B Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.text: The Secretary shall, upon written request, disclose current return information from returns with respect to unearned income from the Internal Revenue Service files to any Federal, State, or local agency administering a program listed in subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: C Restriction on disclosure
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security and the Secretary shall disclose return information under subparagraphs (A) and (B) only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, determining eligibility for, or the correct amount of, benefits under a program listed in subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Programs to which rule applies
children.label: D Programs to which rule applies
i
children.text: a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act;

children.label: ii
children.text: medical assistance provided under a State plan approved under title XIX of the Social Security Act or subsidies provided under section 1860D–14 of such Act;

children.label: iii
children.text: supplemental security income benefits provided under title XVI of the Social Security Act, and federally administered supplementary payments of the type described in section 1616(a) of such Act (including payments pursuant to an agreement entered into under );section 212(a) of Public Law 93–66

children.label: iv
children.text: any benefits provided under a State plan approved under title I, X, XIV, or XVI of the Social Security Act (as those titles apply to Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Virgin Islands);

children.label: v
children.text: unemployment compensation provided under a State law described in ;section 3304 of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: assistance provided under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008;

children.label: vii
children.text: State-administered supplementary payments of the type described in section 1616(a) of the Social Security Act (including payments pursuant to an agreement entered into under );section 212(a) of Public Law 93–66

children.label: I
children.text: any needs-based pension provided under chapter 15 of title 38, United States Code, or under any other law administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs;

children.label: II
children.text: parents’ dependency and indemnity compensation provided under , United States Code;section 1315 of title 38

children.label: III
children.text: health-care services furnished under sections 1710(a)(2)(G), 1710(a)(3), and 1710(b) of such title; and

children.label: IV
children.text: compensation paid under chapter 11 of title 38, United States Code, at the 100 percent rate based solely on unemployability and without regard to the fact that the disability or disabilities are not rated as 100 percent disabling under the rating schedule; and

children.label: ix
children.text: any housing assistance program administered by the Department of Housing and Urban Development that involves initial and periodic review of an applicant’s or participant’s income, except that return information may be disclosed under this clause only on written request by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development and only for use by officers and employees of the Department of Housing and Urban Development with respect to applicants for and participants in such programs.

children.heading: Disclosure of certain return information by Social Security Administration to Federal, State, tribal, and local child support enforcement agencies
children.label: 8 Disclosure of certain return information by Social Security Administration to Federal, State, tribal, and local child support enforcement agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon written request, the Commissioner of Social Security shall disclose directly to officers and employees of a Federal, State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency return information from returns with respect to social security account numbers, net earnings from self-employment (as defined in section 1402), wages (as defined in section 3121(a) or 3401(a)), and payments of retirement income which have been disclosed to the Social Security Administration as provided by paragraph (1) or (5) of this subsection.

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall disclose return information under subparagraph (A) only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing and collecting child support obligations from, and locating, individuals owing such obligations. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “child support obligations” only includes obligations which are being enforced pursuant to the provisions of part D of title IV of the Social Security Act. The information disclosed to any child support enforcement agency under subparagraph (A) with respect to any individual with respect to whom child support obligations are sought to be established or enforced may be disclosed by such agency to any agent of such agency which is under contract with such agency for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing and collecting child support obligations from, and locating, individuals owing such obligations.

children.heading: State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency
children.label: C State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “State, tribal, or local child support enforcement agency” has the same meaning as when used in paragraph (6)(D).

children.heading: Disclosure of alcohol fuel producers to administrators of State alcohol laws
children.label: 9 Disclosure of alcohol fuel producers to administrators of State alcohol laws
A
children.text: the name and address of any person who is qualified to produce alcohol for fuel use under section 5181, and

children.label: B
children.text: the location of any premises to be used by such person in producing alcohol for fuel,

children.heading: Disclosure of certain information to agencies requesting a reduction under subsection (c), (d), (e), or (f) of section 6402
children.label: 10 Disclosure of certain information to agencies requesting a reduction under subsection (c), (d), (e), or (f) of section 6402

children.heading: Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.label: A Return information from Internal Revenue Service
i
children.text: taxpayer identity information with respect to the taxpayer against whom such a reduction was made or not made and with respect to any other person filing a joint return with such taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: the fact that a reduction has been made or has not been made under such subsection with respect to such taxpayer,

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of such reduction,

children.label: iv
children.text: whether such taxpayer filed a joint return, and

children.label: v
children.text: the fact that a payment was made (and the amount of the payment) to the spouse of the taxpayer on the basis of a joint return.

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: B Restriction on use of disclosed information
i
children.text: Any officers and employees of an agency receiving return information under subparagraph (A) shall use such information only for the purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing appropriate agency records, locating any person with respect to whom a reduction under subsection (c), (d), (e), or (f) of section 6402 is sought for purposes of collecting the debt with respect to which the reduction is sought, or in the defense of any litigation or administrative procedure ensuing from a reduction made under subsection (c), (d), (e), or (f) of section 6402.

children.label: ii
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (i), return information disclosed to officers and employees of the Department of Labor may be accessed by agents who maintain and provide technological support to the Department of Labor’s Interstate Connection Network (ICON) solely for the purpose of providing such maintenance and support.

children.label: iii
children.text: The information disclosed to any child support enforcement agency under subparagraph (A) with respect to any individual with respect to whom child support obligations are sought to be established or enforced may be disclosed by such agency to any agent of such agency which is under contract with such agency for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing and collecting child support obligations from, and locating, individuals owing such obligations.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to carry out Federal Employees’ Retirement System
children.label: 11 Disclosure of return information to carry out Federal Employees’ Retirement System

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall, on written request, disclose to the Office of Personnel Management return information from returns with respect to net earnings from self-employment (as defined in section 1402), wages (as defined in section 3121(a) or 3401(a)), and payments of retirement income, which have been disclosed to the Social Security Administration as provided by paragraph (1) or (5).

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall disclose return information under subparagraph (A) only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, the administration of chapters 83 and 84 of title 5, United States Code.

children.heading: Disclosure of certain taxpayer identity information for verification of employment status of medicare beneficiary and spouse of medicare beneficiary
children.label: 12 Disclosure of certain taxpayer identity information for verification of employment status of medicare beneficiary and spouse of medicare beneficiary

children.heading: Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.label: A Return information from Internal Revenue Service
children.text: The Secretary shall, upon written request from the Commissioner of Social Security, disclose to the Commissioner available filing status and taxpayer identity information from the individual master files of the Internal Revenue Service relating to whether any medicare beneficiary identified by the Commissioner was a married individual (as defined in section 7703) for any specified year after 1986, and, if so, the name of the spouse of such individual and such spouse’s TIN.

children.heading: Return information from Social Security Administration
children.label: B Return information from Social Security Administration
i
children.text: The name and TIN of each medicare beneficiary who is identified as having received wages (as defined in section 3401(a)), above an amount (if any) specified by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, from a qualified employer in a previous year.

children.label: I
children.text: the name and TIN of the medicare beneficiary, and

children.label: II
children.text: the name and TIN of the spouse.

children.label: iii
children.text: With respect to each such qualified employer, the name, address, and TIN of the employer and the number of individuals with respect to whom written statements were furnished under section 6051 by the employer with respect to such previous year.

children.heading: Disclosure by Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
children.label: C Disclosure by Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
i
children.text: to the qualified employer referred to in such subparagraph the name and TIN of each individual identified under such subparagraph as having received wages from the employer (hereinafter in this subparagraph referred to as the “employee”) for purposes of determining during what period such employee or the employee’s spouse may be (or have been) covered under a group health plan of the employer and what benefits are or were covered under the plan (including the name, address, and identifying number of the plan),

children.label: I
children.text: the TIN of such employee if benefits were paid under title XVIII of the Social Security Act with respect to the employee during a period in which the plan was a primary plan (as defined in section 1862(b)(2)(A) of the Social Security Act), and

children.label: II
children.text: the TIN of such spouse if benefits were paid under such title with respect to the spouse during such period, and

children.label: iii
children.text: to any agent of such Administrator the information referred to in subparagraph (B) for purposes of carrying out clauses (i) and (ii) on behalf of such Administrator.

children.heading: Restrictions on disclosure
children.label: i Restrictions on disclosure
children.text: Information may be disclosed under this paragraph only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, determining the extent to which any medicare beneficiary is covered under any group health plan.

children.heading: Timely response to requests
children.label: ii Timely response to requests
children.text: Any request made under subparagraph (A) or (B) shall be complied with as soon as possible but in no event later than 120 days after the date the request was made.

children.heading: Medicare beneficiary
children.label: i Medicare beneficiary
children.text: The term “medicare beneficiary” means an individual entitled to benefits under part A, or enrolled under part B, of title XVIII of the Social Security Act, but does not include such an individual enrolled in part A under section 1818.

children.heading: Group health plan
children.label: ii Group health plan
children.text: The term “group health plan” means any group health plan (as defined in section 5000(b)(1)).

children.heading: Qualified employer
children.label: iii Qualified employer
children.text: The term “qualified employer” means, for a calendar year, an employer which has furnished written statements under section 6051 with respect to at least 20 individuals for wages paid in the year.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to carry out the Higher Education Act of 1965
children.label: 13 Disclosure of return information to carry out the Higher Education Act of 1965

children.heading: Applications and recertifications for income-contingent or income-based repayment
children.label: A Applications and recertifications for income-contingent or income-based repayment
i
children.text: Taxpayer identity information.

children.label: ii
children.text: Filing status.

children.label: iii
children.text: Adjusted gross income.

children.label: iv
children.text: Total number of exemptions claimed, if applicable.

children.label: v
children.text: Number of dependents taken into account in determining the credit allowed under section 24.

children.label: vi
children.text: If applicable, the fact that there was no return filed.

children.heading: Discharge of loan based on total and permanent disability
children.label: B Discharge of loan based on total and permanent disability
i
children.text: The return information described in clauses (i), (ii), and (vi) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: ii
children.text: The return information described in subparagraph (C)(ii).

children.label: i
children.text: Return information described in clauses (i) through (vi) of subparagraph (A).

children.label: ii
children.text: The amount of any net earnings from self-employment (as defined in section 1402(a)), wages (as defined in section 3121(a) or 3401(a)), and taxable income from a farming business (as defined in section 263A(e)(4)).

children.label: iii
children.text: Amount of total income tax.

children.label: iv
children.text: Amount of any credit allowed under section 25A.

children.label: v
children.text: Amount of individual retirement account distributions not included in adjusted gross income.

children.label: vi
children.text: Amount of individual retirement account contributions and payments to self-employed SEP, Keogh, and other qualified plans which were deducted from income.

children.label: vii
children.text: Amount of tax-exempt interest received.

children.label: viii
children.text: Amounts from retirement pensions and annuities not included in adjusted gross income.

children.label: I
children.text: Schedule A.

children.label: II
children.text: Schedule B.

children.label: III
children.text: Schedule D.

children.label: IV
children.text: Schedule E.

children.label: V
children.text: Schedule F.

children.label: VI
children.text: Schedule H.

children.label: x
children.text: If applicable, the amount reported on Schedule C (or an equivalent successor schedule) as net profit or loss.

children.heading: Additional uses of disclosed information
children.label: D Additional uses of disclosed information
I
children.text: reducing the net cost of improper payments under such plans, relating to such awards, or relating to such discharges,

children.label: II
children.text: oversight activities by the Office of Inspector General of the Department of Education as authorized by the Inspector General Act of 1978, and

children.label: III
children.text: conducting analyses and forecasts for estimating costs related to such plans, awards, or discharges.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The purposes described in clause (i) shall not include the conduct of criminal investigations or prosecutions.

children.heading: Redisclosure to institutions of higher education, State higher education agencies, and designated scholarship organizations
children.label: iii Redisclosure to institutions of higher education, State higher education agencies, and designated scholarship organizations
I
children.text: An institution of higher education participating in a program under subpart 1 of part A, part C, or part D of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965.

children.label: II
children.text: A State higher education agency.

children.label: III
children.text: A scholarship organization which is an entity designated (prior to the date of the enactment of this clause) by the Secretary of Education under section 483(a)(3)(E) of such Act.

children.heading: Redisclosure to Office of Inspector General, independent auditors, and contractors
children.label: iv Redisclosure to Office of Inspector General, independent auditors, and contractors
I
children.text: may be further disclosed by persons described in subclauses (I), (II), or (III) of clause (iii) or persons designated in the last sentence of clause (iii) to the Office of Inspector General of the Department of Education and independent auditors conducting audits of such person’s administration of the programs for which the return information was received, and

children.label: II
children.text: may be further disclosed by persons described in subclauses (I), (II), or (III) of clause (iii) to contractors of such entities,

children.heading: Redisclosure to family members
children.label: v Redisclosure to family members
children.text: In addition to the purposes for which information is disclosed and used under subparagraphs (A) and (C), or redisclosed under clause (iii), any return information so disclosed or redisclosed may be further disclosed to any individual certified by the Secretary of Education as having provided approval under paragraph (1) or (2) of section 494(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965, as the case may be, for disclosure related to the income-contingent or income-based repayment plan under subparagraph (A) or the eligibility for, and amount of, Federal student financial aid described in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Redisclosure of FAFSA information
children.label: vi Redisclosure of FAFSA information
children.text: Return information received under subparagraph (C) may be redisclosed in accordance with subsection (c) of section 494 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (as in effect on the date of enactment of the COVID-related Tax Relief Act of 2020) to carry out the purposes specified in such subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: is an officer, employee, or contractor, of the Department of Education, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is specifically authorized and designated by the Secretary of Education for purposes of such subparagraph (applied separately with respect to each such subparagraph).

children.heading: Joint returns
children.label: F Joint returns
children.text: In the case of a joint return, any disclosure authorized under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C), and any redisclosure authorized under clause (iii), (iv) (v), or (vi) of subparagraph (D), with respect to an individual shall be treated for purposes of this paragraph as applying with respect to the taxpayer.2So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.2

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to United States Customs Service
children.label: 14 Disclosure of return information to United States Customs Service
A
children.text: ascertaining the correctness of any entry in audits as provided for in section 509 of the Tariff Act of 1930 (), or19 U.S.C. 1509

children.label: B
children.text: other actions to recover any loss of revenue, or to collect duties, taxes, and fees, determined to be due and owing pursuant to such audits.

children.heading: Disclosure of returns filed under section 6050I
children.label: 15 Disclosure of returns filed under section 6050I
A
children.text: any Federal agency,

children.label: B
children.text: any agency of a State or local government, or

children.label: C
children.text: any agency of the government of a foreign country,

children.heading: Disclosure of return information for purposes of administering the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997
children.label: 16 Disclosure of return information for purposes of administering the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon written request available return information (including such information disclosed to the Social Security Administration under paragraph (1) or (5) of this subsection), relating to the amount of wage income (as defined in section 3121(a) or 3401(a)), the name, address, and identifying number assigned under section 6109, of payors of wage income, taxpayer identity (as defined in section 6103(b)(6)), and the occupational status reflected on any return filed by, or with respect to, any individual with respect to whom eligibility for, or the correct amount of, benefits under the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997, is sought to be determined, shall be disclosed by the Commissioner of Social Security, or to the extent not available from the Social Security Administration, by the Secretary, to any duly authorized officer or employee of the Department of the Treasury, or a Trustee or any designated officer or employee of a Trustee (as defined in the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997), or any actuary engaged by a Trustee under the terms of the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997, whose official duties require such disclosure, solely for the purpose of, and to the extent necessary in, determining an individual’s eligibility for, or the correct amount of, benefits under the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997.

children.heading: Disclosure for use in judicial or administrative proceedings
children.label: B Disclosure for use in judicial or administrative proceedings
children.text: Return information disclosed to any person under this paragraph may be disclosed in a judicial or administrative proceeding relating to the determination of an individual’s eligibility for, or the correct amount of, benefits under the District of Columbia Retirement Protection Act of 1997.

children.heading: Disclosure to National Archives and Records Administration
children.label: 17 Disclosure to National Archives and Records Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall, upon written request from the Archivist of the United States, disclose or authorize the disclosure of returns and return information to officers and employees of the National Archives and Records Administration for purposes of, and only to the extent necessary in, the appraisal of records for destruction or retention. No such officer or employee shall, except to the extent authorized by subsection (f), (i)(8), or (p), disclose any return or return information disclosed under the preceding sentence to any person other than to the Secretary, or to another officer or employee of the National Archives and Records Administration whose official duties require such disclosure for purposes of such appraisal.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information for purposes of carrying out a program for advance payment of credit for health insurance costs of eligible individuals
children.label: 18 Disclosure of return information for purposes of carrying out a program for advance payment of credit for health insurance costs of eligible individuals
children.text: The Secretary may disclose to providers of health insurance for any certified individual (as defined in section 7527(c)) return information with respect to such certified individual only to the extent necessary to carry out the program established by section 7527 (relating to advance payment of credit for health insurance costs of eligible individuals).

children.heading: Disclosure of return information for purposes of providing transitional assistance under medicare discount card program
children.label: 19 Disclosure of return information for purposes of providing transitional assistance under medicare discount card program
I
children.text: whether the adjusted gross income, as modified in accordance with specifications of the Secretary of Health and Human Services for purposes of carrying out such section, of such taxpayer and, if applicable, such taxpayer’s spouse, for the applicable year, exceeds the amounts specified by the Secretary of Health and Human Services in order to apply the 100 and 135 percent of the poverty lines under such section, (II) whether the return was a joint return, and (III) the applicable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if applicable, the fact that there is no return filed for such taxpayer for the applicable year.

children.heading: Definition of applicable year
children.label: B Definition of applicable year
children.text: For the purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable year” means the most recent taxable year for which information is available in the Internal Revenue Service’s taxpayer data information systems, or, if there is no return filed for such taxpayer for such year, the prior taxable year.

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: C Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.text: Return information disclosed under this paragraph may be used only for the purposes of determining eligibility for and administering transitional assistance under section 1860D–31 of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to carry out Medicare part B premium subsidy adjustment and part D base beneficiary premium increase
children.label: 20 Disclosure of return information to carry out Medicare part B premium subsidy adjustment and part D base beneficiary premium increase
i
children.text: taxpayer identity information with respect to such taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: the filing status of such taxpayer,

children.label: iii
children.text: the adjusted gross income of such taxpayer,

children.label: iv
children.text: the amounts excluded from such taxpayer’s gross income under sections 135 and 911 to the extent such information is available,

children.label: v
children.text: the interest received or accrued during the taxable year which is exempt from the tax imposed by chapter 1 to the extent such information is available,

children.label: vi
children.text: the amounts excluded from such taxpayer’s gross income by sections 931 and 933 to the extent such information is available,

children.label: vii
children.text: such other information relating to the liability of the taxpayer as is prescribed by the Secretary by regulation as might indicate in the case of a taxpayer who is an individual described in subsection (i)(4)(B)(iii) of section 1839 of the Social Security Act that the amount of the premium of the taxpayer under such section may be subject to adjustment under subsection (i) of such section or increase under section 1860D–13(a)(7) of such Act and the amount of such adjustment, and

children.label: viii
children.text: the taxable year with respect to which the preceding information relates.

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: B Restriction on use of disclosed information

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Return information disclosed under subparagraph (A) may be used by officers, employees, and contractors of the Social Security Administration only for the purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing the appropriate amount of any premium adjustment under such section 1839(i) or increase under such section 1860D–13(a)(7) or for the purpose of resolving taxpayer appeals with respect to any such premium adjustment or increase.

children.label: I
children.text: the taxpayer identity information and the amount of the premium subsidy adjustment or premium increase with respect to a taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) to officers, employees, and contractors of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, to the extent that such disclosure is necessary for the collection of the premium subsidy amount or the increased premium amount,

children.label: II
children.text: the taxpayer identity information and the amount of the premium subsidy adjustment or the increased premium amount with respect to a taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) to officers and employees of the Office of Personnel Management and the Railroad Retirement Board, to the extent that such disclosure is necessary for the collection of the premium subsidy amount or the increased premium amount,

children.label: III
children.text: return information with respect to a taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) to officers and employees of the Department of Health and Human Services to the extent necessary to resolve administrative appeals of such premium subsidy adjustment or increased premium, and

children.label: IV
children.text: return information with respect to a taxpayer described in subparagraph (A) to officers and employees of the Department of Justice for use in judicial proceedings to the extent necessary to carry out the purposes described in clause (i).

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to carry out eligibility requirements for certain programs
children.label: 21 Disclosure of return information to carry out eligibility requirements for certain programs
i
children.text: taxpayer identity information with respect to such taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: the filing status of such taxpayer,

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of individuals for whom a deduction is allowed under section 151 with respect to the taxpayer (including the taxpayer and the taxpayer’s spouse),

children.label: iv
children.text: the modified adjusted gross income (as defined in section 36B) of such taxpayer and each of the other individuals included under clause (iii) who are required to file a return of tax imposed by chapter 1 for the taxable year,

children.label: v
children.text: such other information as is prescribed by the Secretary by regulation as might indicate whether the taxpayer is eligible for such credit or reduction (and the amount thereof), and

children.label: vi
children.text: the taxable year with respect to which the preceding information relates or, if applicable, the fact that such information is not available.

children.heading: Information to exchange and State agencies
children.label: B Information to exchange and State agencies
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services may disclose to an Exchange established under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act or its contractors, or to a State agency administering a State program described in subparagraph (A) or its contractors, any inconsistency between the information provided by the Exchange or State agency to the Secretary and the information provided to the Secretary under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Restriction on use of disclosed information
children.label: C Restriction on use of disclosed information
i
children.text: establishing eligibility for participation in the Exchange, and verifying the appropriate amount of, any credit or reduction described in subparagraph (A),

children.label: ii
children.text: determining eligibility for participation in the State programs described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Disclosure of return information to Department of Health and Human Services for purposes of enhancing Medicare program integrity
children.label: 22 Disclosure of return information to Department of Health and Human Services for purposes of enhancing Medicare program integrity
i
children.text: the taxpayer identity information with respect to such taxpayer;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the delinquent tax debt owed by that taxpayer; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the taxable year to which the delinquent tax debt pertains.

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: Return information disclosed under subparagraph (A) may be used by officers and employees of the Department of Health and Human Services for the purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, establishing the taxpayer’s eligibility for enrollment or reenrollment in the Medicare program, or in any administrative or judicial proceeding relating to, or arising from, a denial of such enrollment or reenrollment, or in determining the level of enhanced oversight to be applied with respect to such taxpayer pursuant to section 1866(j)(3) of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: Delinquent tax debt
children.label: C Delinquent tax debt
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “delinquent tax debt” means an outstanding debt under this title for which a notice of lien has been filed pursuant to section 6323, but the term does not include a debt that is being paid in a timely manner pursuant to an agreement under section 6159 or 7122, or a debt with respect to which a collection due process hearing under section 6330 is requested, pending, or completed and no payment is required.

children.heading: Disclosure of taxpayer identity information
children.label: m Disclosure of taxpayer identity information

children.heading: Tax refunds
children.label: 1 Tax refunds
children.text: The Secretary may disclose taxpayer identity information to the press and other media for purposes of notifying persons entitled to tax refunds when the Secretary, after reasonable effort and lapse of time, has been unable to locate such persons.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary may, upon written request, disclose the mailing address of a taxpayer for use by officers, employees, or agents of a Federal agency for purposes of locating such taxpayer to collect or compromise a Federal claim against the taxpayer in accordance with sections 3711, 3717, and 3718 of title 31.

children.heading: Special rule for consumer reporting agency
children.label: B Special rule for consumer reporting agency
children.text: In the case of an agent of a Federal agency which is a consumer reporting agency (within the meaning of section 603(f) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ()), the mailing address of a taxpayer may be disclosed to such agent under subparagraph (A) only for the purpose of allowing such agent to prepare a commercial credit report on the taxpayer for use by such Federal agency in accordance with sections 3711, 3717, and 3718 of title 31.15 U.S.C. 1681a(f)

children.heading: National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health
children.label: 3 National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health
children.text: Upon written request, the Secretary may disclose the mailing address of taxpayers to officers and employees of the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health solely for the purpose of locating individuals who are, or may have been, exposed to occupational hazards in order to determine the status of their health or to inform them of the possible need for medical care and treatment.

children.heading: Individuals who owe an overpayment of Federal Pell Grants or who have defaulted on student loans administered by the Department of Education
children.label: 4 Individuals who owe an overpayment of Federal Pell Grants or who have defaulted on student loans administered by the Department of Education
i
children.text: who owes an overpayment of a grant awarded to such taxpayer under subpart 1 of part A of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965, or

children.label: I
children.text: made under part B, D, or E of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965, or

children.label: II
children.text: made pursuant to section 3(a)(1) of the Migration and Refugee Assistance Act of 1962 to a student at an institution of higher education,

children.heading: Disclosure to educational institutions, etc.
children.label: B Disclosure to educational institutions, etc.
i
children.text: any lender, or any State or nonprofit guarantee agency, which is participating under part B or D of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any educational institution with which the Secretary of Education has an agreement under subpart 1 of part A, or part D or E, of title IV of such Act,

children.heading: Individuals who have defaulted on student loans administered by the Department of Health and Human Services
children.label: 5 Individuals who have defaulted on student loans administered by the Department of Health and Human Services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon written request by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Secretary may disclose the mailing address of any taxpayer who has defaulted on a loan made under part C of title VII of the Public Health Service Act or under subpart II of part B of title VIII of such Act, for use only by officers, employees, or agents of the Department of Health and Human Services for purposes of locating such taxpayer for purposes of collecting such loan.1

children.heading: Disclosure to schools and eligible lenders
children.label: B Disclosure to schools and eligible lenders
i
children.text: any school with which the Secretary of Health and Human Services has an agreement under subpart II of part C of title VII of the Public Health Service Act or subpart II of part B of title VIII of such Act, or11

children.label: ii
children.text: any eligible lender (within the meaning of section 737(4) of such Act) participating under subpart I of part C of title VII of such Act,11

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon written request pursuant to section 1141 of the Social Security Act, the Secretary shall disclose the mailing address of taxpayers to officers and employees of the Blood Donor Locator Service in the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Restriction on disclosure
children.label: B Restriction on disclosure
children.text: The Secretary shall disclose return information under subparagraph (A) only for purposes of, and to the extent necessary in, assisting under the Blood Donor Locator Service authorized persons (as defined in section 1141(h)(1) of the Social Security Act) in locating blood donors who, as indicated by donated blood or products derived therefrom or by the history of the subsequent use of such blood or blood products, have or may have the virus for acquired immune deficiency syndrome, in order to inform such donors of the possible need for medical care and treatment.

children.heading: Safeguards
children.label: C Safeguards
children.text: The Secretary shall destroy all related blood donor records (as defined in section 1141(h)(2) of the Social Security Act) in the possession of the Department of the Treasury upon completion of their use in making the disclosure required under subparagraph (A), so as to make such records undisclosable.

children.heading: Social security account statement furnished by Social Security Administration
children.label: 7 Social security account statement furnished by Social Security Administration
children.text: Upon written request by the Commissioner of Social Security, the Secretary may disclose the mailing address of any taxpayer who is entitled to receive a social security account statement pursuant to section 1143(c) of the Social Security Act, for use only by officers, employees or agents of the Social Security Administration for purposes of mailing such statement to such taxpayer.

children.heading: Certain other persons
children.label: n Certain other persons
children.text: Pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Secretary, returns and return information may be disclosed to any person, including any person described in section 7513(a), to the extent necessary in connection with the processing, storage, transmission, and reproduction of such returns and return information, the programming, maintenance, repair, testing, and procurement of equipment, and the providing of other services, for purposes of tax administration.

children.heading: Disclosure of returns and return information with respect to certain taxes
children.label: o Disclosure of returns and return information with respect to certain taxes

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Returns and return information with respect to taxes imposed by subtitle E (relating to taxes on alcohol, tobacco, and firearms) shall be open to inspection by or disclosure to officers and employees of a Federal agency whose official duties require such inspection or disclosure.

children.heading: Use in certain proceedings
children.label: B Use in certain proceedings
children.text: Returns and return information disclosed to a Federal agency under subparagraph (A) may be used in an action or proceeding (or in preparation for such action or proceeding) brought under section 625 of the American Jobs Creation Act of 2004 for the collection of any unpaid assessment or penalty arising under such Act.

children.heading: Taxes imposed by chapter 35
children.label: 2 Taxes imposed by chapter 35
children.text: Returns and return information with respect to taxes imposed by chapter 35 (relating to taxes on wagering) shall, notwithstanding any other provision of this section, be open to inspection by or disclosure only to such person or persons and for such purpose or purposes as are prescribed by section 4424.

children.heading: Taxes imposed by section 4481
children.label: 3 Taxes imposed by section 4481
children.text: Returns and return information with respect to taxes imposed by section 4481 shall be open to inspection by or disclosure to officers and employees of United States Customs and Border Protection of the Department of Homeland Security whose official duties require such inspection or disclosure for purposes of administering such section.

children.heading: Manner, time, and place of inspections
children.label: 1 Manner, time, and place of inspections
children.text: Requests for the inspection or disclosure of a return or return information and such inspection or disclosure shall be made in such manner and at such time and place as shall be prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reproduction of returns
children.label: A Reproduction of returns
children.text: A reproduction or certified reproduction of a return shall, upon written request, be furnished to any person to whom disclosure or inspection of such return is authorized under this section. A reasonable fee may be prescribed for furnishing such reproduction or certified reproduction.

children.heading: Disclosure of return information
children.label: B Disclosure of return information
children.text: Return information disclosed to any person under the provisions of this title may be provided in the form of written documents, reproductions of such documents, films or photoimpressions, or electronically produced tapes, disks, or records, or by any other mode or means which the Secretary determines necessary or appropriate. A reasonable fee may be prescribed for furnishing such return information.

children.heading: Use of reproductions
children.label: C Use of reproductions
children.text: Any reproduction of any return, document, or other matter made in accordance with this paragraph shall have the same legal status as the original, and any such reproduction shall, if properly authenticated, be admissible in evidence in any judicial or administrative proceeding as if it were the original, whether or not the original is in existence.

children.heading: System of recordkeeping
children.label: A System of recordkeeping
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by this paragraph, the Secretary shall maintain a permanent system of standardized records or accountings of all requests for inspection or disclosure of returns and return information (including the reasons for and dates of such requests) and of returns and return information inspected or disclosed under this section and section 6104(c). Notwithstanding the provisions of , United States Code, the Secretary shall not be required to maintain a record or accounting of requests for inspection or disclosure of returns and return information, or of returns and return information inspected or disclosed, under the authority of subsection (c), (e), (f)(5), (h)(1), (3)(A), or (4), (i)(4), or (8)(A)(ii), (k)(1), (2), (6), (8), or (9), ()(1), (4)(B), (5), (7), (8), (9), (10), (11), (12), (13)(D)(iv), (13)(D)(v), (13)(D)(vi) (14), (15), (16), (17), or (18), (m), or (n). The records or accountings required to be maintained under this paragraph shall be available for examination by the Joint Committee on Taxation or the Chief of Staff of such joint committee. Such record or accounting shall also be available for examination by such person or persons as may be, but only to the extent, authorized to make such examination under , United States Code.section 552a(c) of title 5section 552a(c)(3) of title 5l2

children.heading: Report by the Secretary
children.label: B Report by the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall, within 90 days after the close of each calendar year, furnish to the Joint Committee on Taxation a report with respect to, or summary of, the records or accountings described in subparagraph (A) in such form and containing such information as such joint committee or the Chief of Staff of such joint committee may designate. Such report or summary shall not, however, include a record or accounting of any request by the President under subsection (g) for, or the disclosure in response to such request of, any return or return information with respect to any individual who, at the time of such request, was an officer or employee of the executive branch of the Federal Government. Such report or summary, or any part thereof, may be disclosed by such joint committee to such persons and for such purposes as the joint committee may, by record vote of a majority of the members of the joint committee, determine.

children.label: I
children.text: requests for disclosure of returns and return information,

children.label: II
children.text: instances in which returns and return information were disclosed pursuant to such requests or otherwise,

children.label: III
children.text: taxpayers whose returns, or return information with respect to whom, were disclosed pursuant to such requests, and

children.label: ii
children.text: describes the general purposes for which such requests were made.

children.label: A
children.text: establish and maintain, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, a permanent system of standardized records with respect to any request, the reason for such request, and the date of such request made by or of it and any disclosure of return or return information made by or to it;

children.label: B
children.text: establish and maintain, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, a secure area or place in which such returns or return information shall be stored;

children.label: C
children.text: restrict, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, access to the returns or return information only to persons whose duties or responsibilities require access and to whom disclosure may be made under the provisions of this title;

children.label: D
children.text: provide such other safeguards which the Secretary determines (and which he prescribes in regulations) to be necessary or appropriate to protect the confidentiality of the returns or return information;

children.label: E
children.text: furnish a report to the Secretary, at such time and containing such information as the Secretary may prescribe, which describes the procedures established and utilized by such agency, body, or commission, the Government Accountability Office, or the Congressional Budget Office for ensuring the confidentiality of returns and return information required by this paragraph; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an agency, body, or commission described in subsection (d), (i)(3)(B)(i), (k)(10), or ()(6), (7), (8), (9), or (16), any appropriate State officer (as defined in section 6104(c)), or any other person described in subsection (k)(10) or (15) or subsection ()(6), (8), (10), (13)(A), (13)(B), (13)(C), (13)(D)(i), (16), (18), (19), or (20) return to the Secretary such returns or return information (along with any copies made therefrom) or make such returns or return information undisclosable in any manner and furnish a written report to the Secretary describing such manner,ll

children.label: I
children.text: return to the Secretary such returns or return information (along with any copies made therefrom),

children.label: II
children.text: otherwise make such returns or return information undisclosable, or

children.label: III
children.text: to the extent not so returned or made undisclosable, ensure that the conditions of subparagraphs (A), (B), (C), (D), and (E) of this paragraph continue to be met with respect to such returns or return information, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of the Department of Health and Human Services for purposes of subsection (m)(6), destroy all such return information upon completion of its use in providing the notification for which the information was obtained, so as to make such information undisclosable;

children.heading: Report on procedures and safeguards
children.label: 5 Report on procedures and safeguards
children.text: After the close of each calendar year, the Secretary shall furnish to each committee described in subsection (f)(1) a report which describes the procedures and safeguards established and utilized by such agencies, bodies, or commissions, the Government Accountability Office, and the Congressional Budget Office for ensuring the confidentiality of returns and return information as required by this subsection. Such report shall also describe instances of deficiencies in, and failure to establish or utilize, such procedures.

children.heading: Audit by Comptroller General
children.label: A Audit by Comptroller General
children.text: The Comptroller General may audit the procedures and safeguards established by such agencies, bodies, or commissions and the Congressional Budget Office pursuant to this subsection to determine whether such safeguards and procedures meet the requirements of this subsection and ensure the confidentiality of returns and return information. The Comptroller General shall notify the Secretary before any such audit is conducted.

children.heading: Records of inspection and reports by the Comptroller General
children.label: B Records of inspection and reports by the Comptroller General
i
children.text: maintain a permanent system of standardized records and accountings of returns and return information inspected by officers and employees of the Government Accountability Office under subsection (i)(8)(A)(ii) and shall, within 90 days after the close of each calendar year, furnish to the Secretary a report with respect to, or summary of, such records or accountings in such form and containing such information as the Secretary may prescribe, and

children.label: ii
children.text: furnish an annual report to each committee described in subsection (f) and to the Secretary setting forth his findings with respect to any audit conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Administrative review
children.label: 7 Administrative review
children.text: The Secretary shall by regulations prescribe procedures which provide for administrative review of any determination under paragraph (4) that any agency, body, or commission described in subsection (d) has failed to meet the requirements of such paragraph.

children.heading: Safeguards
children.label: A Safeguards
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, no return or return information shall be disclosed after , to any officer or employee of any State which requires a taxpayer to attach to, or include in, any State tax return a copy of any portion of his Federal return, or information reflected on such Federal return, unless such State adopts provisions of law which protect the confidentiality of the copy of the Federal return (or portion thereof) attached to, or the Federal return information reflected on, such State tax return.December 31, 1978

children.heading: Disclosure of returns or return information in State returns
children.label: B Disclosure of returns or return information in State returns
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) or paragraph (9) shall be construed to prohibit the disclosure by an officer or employee of any State of any copy of any portion of a Federal return or any information on a Federal return which is required to be attached or included in a State return to another officer or employee of such State (or political subdivision of such State) if such disclosure is specifically authorized by State law.

children.heading: Disclosure to contractors and other agents
children.label: 9 Disclosure to contractors and other agents
A
children.text: has requirements in effect which require each such contractor or other agent which would have access to returns or return information to provide safeguards (within the meaning of paragraph (4)) to protect the confidentiality of such returns or return information,

children.label: B
children.text: agrees to conduct an on-site review every 3 years (or a mid-point review in the case of contracts or agreements of less than 3 years in duration) of each contractor or other agent to determine compliance with such requirements,

children.label: C
children.text: submits the findings of the most recent review conducted under subparagraph (B) to the Secretary as part of the report required by paragraph (4)(E), and

children.label: D
children.text: certifies to the Secretary for the most recent annual period that such contractor or other agent is in compliance with all such requirements.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: q Regulations
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to prescribe such other regulations as are necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
